devanagari: सांबविवाहः बलरामेण हस्तिनापुरकर्षणं च । श्रीशुक उवाच । दुर्योधनसुतां राजन् लक्ष्मणां समितिंजयः । स्वयंवरस्थामहरत् सांबो जाम्बवतीसुतः ॥ ...
english_devnagari: sāṃbavivāhaḥ balarāmeṇa hastināpurakarṣaṇaṃ ca śrīśuka uvāca duryodhanasutāṃ rājan lakṣmaṇāṃ samitiṃjayaḥ svayaṃvarasthāmaharat sāṃbo jāmbavatīsutaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
O King Sāmba, the son of Jāmbavatī, who was always victorious in battles, carried away Duryodhana’s daughter Lakṣmaṇā, at the time of her svayaṃvara.

devanagari: कौरवाः कुपिता ऊचुः दुर्विनीतोऽयमर्भकः । कदर्थीकृत्य नः कन्यां अकामां अहरद् बलात् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: kauravāḥ kupitā ūcuḥ durvinīto kadarthīkṛtya naḥ kanyāṃ akāmāṃ aharad balāt

translation: Kauravas were extremely enraged at this, “This is a naughty boy. Setting us at nought, he per force carried away our daughter who did not want him (as a suitor).

devanagari: बध्नीतेमं दुर्विनीतं किं करिष्यन्ति वृष्णयः । येऽस्मत् प्रसादोपचितां दत्तां नो भुञ्जते महीम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: badhnītemaṃ durvinītaṃ kiṃ kariṣyanti vṛṣṇayaḥ ye

translation: Arrest and keep in confinement this unruly, wicked fellow. What can the Vṛṣṇis do? They are enjoying that territory conferred upon them by us and that too through our grace (we are the real masters of that land).

devanagari: निगृहीतं सुतं श्रुत्वा यद्येष्यन्तीह वृष्णयः । भग्नदर्पाः शमं यान्ति प्राणा इव सुसंयताः ॥ ४ ॥

translation: If, after hearing of the captivity of their son, they come here, the Vṛṣṇis will sue for peace with their pride humbled down like senses brought under control by breathcontrol (prāṇāyāma).”[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
v.l. like serpents whose fangs are removed,—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: इति कर्णः शलो भूरिः यज्ञकेतुः सुयोधनः । साम्बमारेभिरे बद्धुं कुरुवृद्धानुमोदिताः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: iti karṇaḥ śalo bhūriḥ yajñaketuḥ suyodhanaḥ sāmbamārebhire baddhuṃ kuruvṛddhānumoditāḥ

translation:
With this resolve, Karṇa, Śala, Bhūri, Yajñaketu and Duryodhana
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text:]
5(A). Along with Duḥśāsana, Śakuni who always gave wrong and wicked advice—all these disregarded Vidura and glorious Gāndhārī... and with the approval of the Kaurava elders (like Bhīṣma) attempted to bind Sāmba.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वानुधावतः साम्बो धार्तराष्ट्रान् महारथः । प्रगृह्य रुचिरं चापं तस्थौ सिंह इवैकलः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvānudhāvataḥ sāmbo dhārtarāṣṭrān mahārathaḥ pragṛhya ruciraṃ cāpaṃ tasthau siṃha ivaikalaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Noticing that he was being pursued by Kauravas, the great chariot-warrior Sāmba, took up his beautiful bow and stood defiantly like a single lion turned at bay.

devanagari: तं ते जिघृक्षवः क्रुद्धाः तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति भाषिणः । आसाद्य धन्विनो बाणैः कर्णाग्रण्यः समाकिरन् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ te jighṛkṣavaḥ kruddhāḥ tiṣṭha tiṣṭheti bhāṣiṇaḥ āsādya dhanvino bāṇaiḥ karṇāgraṇyaḥ samākiran

[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: Enraged and eager to take him captive, they shouted, ‘Stop’, ‘Stop’. And under the leadership of Karṇa, those archers covered him with a volley of arrows.

devanagari: सोऽपविद्धः कुरुश्रेष्ठ कुरुभिर्यदुनन्दनः । नामृष्यत् तदचिन्त्यार्भः सिंह क्षुद्रमृगैरिव ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: so nāmṛṣyat tadacintyārbhaḥ siṃha kṣudramṛgairiva
translation: Though surrounded by Kauravas, that scion of the Yadu race, the son of the incomprehensible Lord Kṛṣṇa, did not tolerate them like a lion attacked by insignificant beasts.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
v.l. He did not put up with the insult like a serpent beaten by a stick.

devanagari: विस्फूर्ज्य रुचिरं चापं सर्वान् विव्याध सायकैः । कर्णादीन् षड्रथान् वीरः तावद्भिर्युगपत् पृथक् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: visphūrjya ruciraṃ cāpaṃ sarvān vivyādha sāyakaiḥ karṇādīn ṣaḍrathān vīraḥ tāvadbhiryugapat pṛthak

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Twanging his beautiful bow, he pierced simultaneously each of the six chariot-warriors like Karṇa and others, with equal number of shafts. He struck the four horses; with four arrows, the charioteers and the great archers in the chariots with one. All of them applauded his exploit.

devanagari: चतुर्भिश्चतुरो वाहान् एकैकेन च सारथीन् । रथिनश्च महेष्वासान् तस्य तत्तेऽभ्यपूजयन् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: caturbhiścaturo vāhān ekaikena ca sārathīn rathinaśca maheṣvāsān tasya tatte
translation: Twanging his beautiful bow, he pierced simultaneously each of the six chariot-warriors like Karṇa and others, with equal number of shafts. He struck the four horses; with four arrows, the charioteers and the great archers in the chariots with one. All of them applauded his exploit.

devanagari: तं तु ते विरथं चक्रुः चत्वारश्चतुरो हयान् । एकस्तु सारथिं जघ्ने चिच्छेदान्यः शरासनम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tu te virathaṃ cakruḥ catvāraścaturo hayān ekastu sārathiṃ jaghne cicchedānyaḥ śarāsanam

translation: Attacking him simultaneously, they deprived him of his chariot, each Kaurava warrior killing one of his four horses one his charioteer and the remaining warrior snapped his bow.

devanagari: तं बद्ध्वा विरथीकृत्य कृच्छ्रेण कुरवो युधि । कुमारं स्वस्य कन्यां च स्वपुरं जयिनोऽविशन् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ baddhvā virathīkṛtya kṛcchreṇa kuravo yudhi kumāraṃ svasya kanyāṃ ca svapuraṃ jayino

translation: With great difficulty the Kauravas dislodged him from the chariot and took him captive. Victorious they entered their capital, with the boy as well as the maiden.

devanagari: तच्छ्रुत्वा नारदोक्तेन राजन् सञ्जातमन्यवः । कुरून् प्रत्युद्यमं चक्रुः उग्रसेनप्रचोदिताः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tacchrutvā nāradoktena rājan sañjātamanyavaḥ kurūn pratyudyamaṃ cakruḥ ugrasenapracoditāḥ

translation: Hearing the news from Nārada’s report, Yādavas got enraged, O King. And under the direction of Ugrasena, they made preparations of an expedition against the Kauravas.

devanagari: सान्त्वयित्वा तु तान् रामः सन्नद्धान् वृष्णिपुङ्गवान् । नैच्छय् कुरूणां वृष्णीनां कलिं कलिमलापहः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sāntvayitvā tu tān rāmaḥ sannaddhān vṛṣṇipuṅgavān naicchay kurūṇāṃ vṛṣṇīnāṃ kaliṃ kalimalāpahaḥ
translation: But Balarāma pacified the Vṛṣṇi warriors who made lull preparations of war. He was disposed to wash away the dirt (bitterness) of quarrels. He did not wish that there should be a feud between Kauravas and Vṛṣṇis.

devanagari: जगाम हास्तिनपुरं रथेनादित्यवर्चसा । ब्राह्मणैः कुलवृद्धैश्च वृतश्चन्द्र इव ग्रहैः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: jagāma hāstinapuraṃ rathenādityavarcasā brāhmaṇaiḥ kulavṛddhaiśca vṛtaścandra iva grahaiḥ

translation: In a chariot resplendent like the sun, he went to Hastināpura. He was accompanied with Brāhmaṇas and elderly persons of the family like the moon surrounded by planets.

devanagari: गत्वा गजाह्वयं रामो बाह्योपवनमास्थितः । उद्धवं प्रेषयामास धृतराष्ट्रं बुभुत्सया ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: gatvā gajāhvayaṃ rāmo bāhyopavanamāsthitaḥ uddhavaṃ preṣayāmāsa dhṛtarāṣṭraṃ bubhutsayā
translation: Arriving at Hastināpura, he camped in a park outside the town. He deputed Uddhava to Dhṛtarāṣṭra to ascertain what they intended to do in the matter.

devanagari: सोऽभिवन्द्याम्बिकापुत्रं भीष्मं द्रोणं च बाह्लिकम् । दुर्योधनं च विधिवद् राममागतमब्रवीत् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: so duryodhanaṃ ca vidhivad rāmamāgatamabravīt
translation: Uddhava formally paid respects to Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Bāhlīka and Duryodhana and intimated to them the arrival of Balarāma.

devanagari: तेऽतिप्रीतास्तमाकर्ण्य प्राप्तं रामं सुहृत्तमम् । तमर्चयित्वाभिययुः सर्वे मङ्गलपाणयः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: te tamarcayitvābhiyayuḥ sarve maṅgalapāṇayaḥ

translation: They were highly pleased to hear that Balarāma, their best friend had arrived. They warmly received Uddhava and taking into their hands auspicious presents, they went to Balarāma.

devanagari: तं सङ्गम्य यथान्यायं गामर्घ्यं च न्यवेदयन् । तेषां ये तत्प्रभावज्ञाः प्रणेमुः शिरसा बलम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ saṅgamya yathānyāyaṃ gāmarghyaṃ ca nyavedayan teṣāṃ ye tatprabhāvajñāḥ praṇemuḥ śirasā balam
translation: Approaching him they received him with due formalities and honours offering him arghya and a cow. Those of them who knew of his prowess bowed down Balarāma with their heads.

devanagari: बन्धून् कुशलिनः श्रुत्वा पृष्ट्वा शिवमनामयम् । परस्परमथो रामो बभाषेऽविक्लवं वचः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: bandhūn kuśalinaḥ śrutvā pṛṣṭvā śivamanāmayam parasparamatho rāmo babhāṣe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Hearing that their respective relatives were hale and hearty and enquiring after their welfare and health and such friendly talk, Balarāma declared fearlessly.

devanagari: उग्रसेनः क्षितीशेशो यद् व आज्ञापयत् प्रभुः । तद् अव्यग्रधियः श्रुत्वा कुरुध्वं माविलम्बितम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ugrasenaḥ kṣitīśeśo yad va ājñāpayat prabhuḥ tad avyagradhiyaḥ śrutvā kurudhvaṃ māvilambitam

translation: ‘Listen attentively to what His majesty Ugrasena, King of kings, commanded you: Carry out his orders without delay.

devanagari: यद् यूयं बहवस्त्वेकं जित्वाधर्मेण धार्मिकम् । अबध्नीताथ तन्मृष्ये बन्धूनामैक्यकाम्यया ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yad yūyaṃ bahavastvekaṃ jitvādharmeṇa dhārmikam abadhnītātha tanmṛṣye bandhūnāmaikyakāmyayā

translation: (We hear) that a great many of you unjustly (according to the rules of warfare) combined and vanquished Sāmba who was fighting singlehandedly (as per canons of dharma-yuddha) and have taken him captive. With the desire of maintaining amity among relatives, I do tolerate this. (But now immediately bring him to us along with the bride).”

devanagari: वीर्यशौर्यबलोन्नद्धं आत्मशक्तिसमं वचः । कुरवो बलदेवस्य निशम्योचुः प्रकोपिताः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: vīryaśauryabalonnaddhaṃ ātmaśaktisamaṃ vacaḥ kuravo baladevasya niśamyocuḥ prakopitāḥ
translation: O Parīkṣit, Kouravas [Kauravas?] became deeply indignant to hear the speech of Balarāma who was endowed with immense power, prowess, energy and whose words expressed his inner strength. They said:

devanagari: अहो महच्चित्रमिदं कालगत्या दुरत्यया । आरुरुक्षत्युपानद् वै शिरो मुकुटसेवितम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: aho mahaccitramidaṃ kālagatyā duratyayā ārurukṣatyupānad vai śiro mukuṭasevitam

translation: “O! How wonderful it is! Due to the inexorable force of time, a shoe covets to ride over the head that is served by a crown.

devanagari: एते यौनेन संबद्धाः सहशय्यासनाशनाः । वृष्णयस्तुल्यतां नीता अस्मद् दत्तनृपासनाः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ete yaunena saṃbaddhāḥ sahaśayyāsanāśanāḥ vṛṣṇayastulyatāṃ nītā asmad dattanṛpāsanāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ nī class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ nī class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ nī class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ nī class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ nī class 1 verb]

translation: These Vṛṣṇis are related to us by conjugal ties (through Kuntī’s marriage in our family). Condescended to share with us the bed, seat and food, the Vṛṣṇis are raised to royal status like us and are favoured with a royal throne.

devanagari: चामरव्यजने शङ्खं आतपत्रं च पाण्डुरम् । किरीटमासनं शय्यां भुञ्जन्त्यस्मदुपेक्षया ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: cāmaravyajane śaṅkhaṃ ātapatraṃ ca pāṇḍuram kirīṭamāsanaṃ śayyāṃ bhuñjantyasmadupekṣayā
translation: It is due to our indulgence that they enjoy the royal insignia like chowries, fans, a conch, a white umbrella, a crown, a throne and a bed.

devanagari: अलं यदूनां नरदेवलाञ्छनैः । दातुः प्रतीपैः फणिनामिवामृतम् । येऽस्मत्प्रसादोपचिता हि यादवा । आज्ञापयन्त्यद्य गतत्रपा बत ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: alaṃ yadūnāṃ naradevalāñchanaiḥ dātuḥ pratīpaiḥ phaṇināmivāmṛtam ye ājñāpayantyadya gatatrapā bata

translation: Now enough of those royal insignia to Yadus who go against their very donors like serpents made to drink nectar (Let us deprive the Yadus of those emblems) who prospered due to our grace are now giving orders to us shamelessly.

devanagari: कथमिन्द्रोऽपि कुरुभिः भीष्मद्रोणार्जुनादिभिः । अदत्तमवरुन्धीत सिंहग्रस्तमिवोरणः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: kathamindro adattamavarundhīta siṃhagrastamivoraṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ gras class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ gras class 1 verb]

translation: How could even Indra dare to take what is not given to him by Kauravas like Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Arjuna and others? Can a ram dare to touch what is in the grasp of a lion (unless he gives it up out of grace)?”

devanagari: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाच । जन्मबन्धुश्रीयोन्नद्ध मदास्ते भरतर्षभ । आश्राव्य रामं दुर्वाच्यं असभ्याः पुरमाविशन् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībādarāyaṇiruvāca janmabandhuśrīyonnaddha madāste bharatarṣabha āśrāvya rāmaṃ durvācyaṃ asabhyāḥ puramāviśan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Being elated with inordinate pride of their noble birth, their high relations, prosperity, those wicked unmannerly Kauravas addressed unspeakable words within the hearing of Balarāma and returned to their city.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा कुरूनां दौःशील्यं श्रुत्वावाच्यानि चाच्युतः । अवोचत् कोपसंरब्धो दुष्प्रेक्ष्यः प्रहसन् मुहुः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā kurūnāṃ dauḥśīlyaṃ śrutvāvācyāni cācyutaḥ avocat kopasaṃrabdho duṣprekṣyaḥ prahasan muhuḥ
translation: Observing the wicked behaviour of Kauravas and hearing their unspeakable reproaches, Balarāma became indignant with wrath and was terrific to look at. Laughing loudly, he repeated to himself:

devanagari: नूनं नानामदोन्नद्धाः शान्तिं नेच्छन्त्यसाधवः । तेषां हि प्रशमो दण्डः पशूनां लगुडो यथा ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ nānāmadonnaddhāḥ śāntiṃ necchantyasādhavaḥ teṣāṃ hi praśamo daṇḍaḥ paśūnāṃ laguḍo yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [nominative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation: “Verily these wicked persons grown madly arrogant with pride of various kinds, do not desire to have peacefulness. Just as (beating with) a stick is the sure remedy to tame a beast, sound thrashing is the only means to bring these round.

devanagari: अहो यदून् सुसंरब्धाम् कृष्णं च कुपितं शनैः । सान्त्वयित्वाहमेतेषां शममिच्छन् इहागतः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: aho yadūn susaṃrabdhām kṛṣṇaṃ ca kupitaṃ śanaiḥ sāntvayitvāhameteṣāṃ śamamicchan ihāgataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ kup class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ kup class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ kup class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation: It is after slowly appeasing the excited Yadus and indignant Kṛṣṇa that I came here with the intent of bringing about a peace with the Kurus.

devanagari: त इमे मन्दमतयः कलहाभिरताः खलाः । तं मामवज्ञाय मुहुः दुर्भाषान् मानिनोऽब्रुवन् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: ta ime mandamatayaḥ kalahābhiratāḥ khalāḥ taṃ māmavajñāya muhuḥ durbhāṣān mānino
translation: These dull-witted wicked fellows are naturally inclined to quarrelling. These arrogant fellows have not only treated me scornfully but have used extremely reproachful words to me.

devanagari: नोग्रसेनः किल विभुः भोजवृष्ण्यन्धकेश्वरः । शक्रादयो लोकपाला यस्यादेशानुवर्तिनः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: nograsenaḥ kila vibhuḥ bhojavṛṣṇyandhakeśvaraḥ śakrādayo lokapālā yasyādeśānuvartinaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: To them King Ugrasena, the ruler of Bhoja, Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans is not a sovereign ruler. Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of goddess Lakṣmī whose orders are obeyed by Indra and other Lords of Spheres, He by whom the celestial assembly Suḍharmā is occupied and the heavenly tree Pārijāta is brought (on the earth) and is enjoyed (according to these fellows) does not deserve a high royal seat.

devanagari: सुधर्माऽक्रम्यते येन पारिजातोऽमराङ्घ्रिपः । आनीय भुज्यते सोऽसौ न किलाध्यासनार्हणः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: sudharmā ānīya bhujyate so
translation: To them King Ugrasena, the ruler of Bhoja, Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans is not a sovereign ruler. Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of goddess Lakṣmī whose orders are obeyed by Indra and other Lords of Spheres, He by whom the celestial assembly Suḍharmā is occupied and the heavenly tree Pārijāta is brought (on the earth) and is enjoyed (according to these fellows) does not deserve a high royal seat.

devanagari: यस्य पादयुगं साक्षात् श्रीरुपास्तेऽखिलेश्वरी । स नार्हति किल श्रीशो नरदेवपरिच्छदान् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: yasya pādayugaṃ sākṣāt śrīrupāste sa nārhati kila śrīśo naradevaparicchadān
translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa whose pair of feet are directly worshipped by goddess Śrī in person, the Controller of all beings does not deserve the royal insignia (according to them).

devanagari: यस्याङ्घ्रिपङ्कजरजोऽखिललोकपालैः । मौल्युत्तमैर्धृतमुपासिततीर्थतीर्थम् । ब्रह्मा भवोऽहमपि यस्य कलाः कलायाः । श्रीश्चोद्वहेम चिरमस्य नृप...
english_devnagari: yasyāṅghripaṅkajarajo maulyuttamairdhṛtamupāsitatīrthatīrtham brahmā bhavo śrīścodvahema ciramasya nṛpāsanaṃ kva
translation: The royal throne is extremely insignificant to him the dust on whose lotus feet īs borne on the crowns of their heads by all the Lords of the various worlds (in the universe) and which sanctify sacred waters (like those of the Gaṅgā) resorted to by holy men; and we—that is god Brahmā, Śiva, I (Saṅkarṣaṇa), and even goddess Śrī are but a part of His ray and always bear those particles of dust (on our heads).

devanagari: भुञ्जते कुरुभिर्दत्तं भूखण्डं वृष्णयः किल । उपानहः किल वयं स्वयं तु कुरवः शिरः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhuñjate kurubhirdattaṃ bhūkhaṇḍaṃ vṛṣṇayaḥ kila upānahaḥ kila vayaṃ svayaṃ tu kuravaḥ śiraḥ
translation: We, Vṛṣṇis, enjoy the piece of land granted to us by Kurus! And we are nothing better than shoes and the Kauravas themselves the head!

devanagari: अहो ऐश्वर्यमत्तानां मत्तानामिव मानिनाम् । असंबद्धा गिरो रुक्षाः कः सहेतानुशासीता ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: aho aiśvaryamattānāṃ mattānāmiva māninām asaṃbaddhā giro rukṣāḥ kaḥ sahetānuśāsītā

translation: What person capable of meting out punishment to others can tolerate the harsh and irrelevant words of those who are drunk with power and wealth and have become intoxicated and insolent?

devanagari: अद्य निष्कौरवं पृथ्वीं करिष्यामीत्यमर्षितः । गृहीत्वा हलमुत्तस्थौ दहन्निव जगत्त्रयम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: adya niṣkauravaṃ pṛthvīṃ kariṣyāmītyamarṣitaḥ gṛhītvā halamuttasthau dahanniva jagattrayam

translation: Today I shall rid the earth of all the Kurus”. With this determination, enraged as he was, Balarāma lifted up his plough as if to burn down the three worlds.

devanagari: लाङ्गलाग्रेण नगरं उद्विदार्य गजाह्वयम् । विचकर्ष स गङ्गायां प्रहरिष्यन्नमर्षितः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: lāṅgalāgreṇa nagaraṃ udvidārya gajāhvayam vicakarṣa sa gaṅgāyāṃ prahariṣyannamarṣitaḥ

translation: With the tip of the plough-share, he lifted up the whole city of Hastināpura and angrily began to drag it intending to throw it into the river Gaṅgā.

devanagari: जलयानमिवाघूर्णं गङ्गायां नगरं पतत् । आकृष्यमाणमालोक्य कौरवाः जातसंभ्रमाः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: jalayānamivāghūrṇaṃ gaṅgāyāṃ nagaraṃ patat ākṛṣyamāṇamālokya kauravāḥ jātasaṃbhramāḥ

translation: The city that was being dragged and falling into the Gaṅgā, began to rock like a boat. Finding the city so dragged, Kauravas were utterly confounded with consternation.

devanagari: तमेव शरणं जग्मुः सकुटुम्बा जिजीविषवः । सलक्ष्मणं पुरस्कृत्य साम्बं प्राञ्जलयः प्रभुम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: tameva śaraṇaṃ jagmuḥ sakuṭumbā jijīviṣavaḥ salakṣmaṇaṃ puraskṛtya sāmbaṃ prāñjalayaḥ prabhum
translation: Anxious to save their lives along with those of their families, they placed forward Sāmba and (his spouse) Lakṣmaṇā and with folded palms sought shelter of that very Lord (Balarāma whom they had insulted).

devanagari: राम रामाखिलाधार प्रभावं न विदाम ते । मूढानां नः कुबुद्धीनां क्षन्तुमर्हस्यतिक्रमम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: rāma rāmākhilādhāra prabhāvaṃ na vidāma te mūḍhānāṃ naḥ kubuddhīnāṃ kṣantumarhasyatikramam
translation: ‘O Balarāma, O Rāma. You are the support of all. We did not know your prowess. It behoves you to pardon the transgression committed by us who have been foolish and wicked-minded.

devanagari: स्थित्युत्पत्त्यप्ययानां त्वमेको हेतुर्निराश्रयः । लोकान् क्रीडनकानीश क्रीडतस्ते वदन्ति हि ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: sthityutpattyapyayānāṃ tvameko heturnirāśrayaḥ lokān krīḍanakānīśa krīḍataste vadanti hi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: (You being the substratum of all) you yourself are perfectly independent but you are the cause of the creation, maintenance and destruction of the universe. It is true when they say that the three worlds are your toys and you are the player.

devanagari: त्वमेव मूर्ध्नीदमनन्त लीलया । भूमण्डलं बिभर्षि सहस्रमूर्धन् । अन्ते च यः स्वात्मनि रुद्धविश्वः । शेषेऽद्वितीयः परिशिष्यमाणः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: tvameva mūrdhnīdamananta līlayā bhūmaṇḍalaṃ bibharṣi sahasramūrdhan ante ca yaḥ svātmani ruddhaviśvaḥ śeṣe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ śiṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śiṣ class 7 verb]

translation: (Referring to the original state of Balarāma as the Serpent Śeṣa, the support of the worlds:) It is you! O Ananta (Serpent Śeṣa) of thousand heads, who easily hold up this globe of the earth on your head. At the end of the universe, you withdraw the universe within yourself and contain it. And repose on the cosmic waters as the only remaining unabsorbed existence without a second.

devanagari: कोपस्तेऽखिलशिक्षार्थं न द्वेषान्न च मत्सरात् । बिभ्रतो भगवन् सत्त्वं स्थितिपालनतत्परः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: kopaste bibhrato bhagavan sattvaṃ sthitipālanatatparaḥ
translation: O glorious Lord! You assume the Sattva guṇa with the intention of maintaining and protecting the world. Hence your anger is only for teaching a lesson to all and not out of hatred or jealousy.

devanagari: नमस्ते सर्वभूतात्मन् सर्वशक्तिधराव्यय । विश्वकर्मन् नमस्तेऽस्तु त्वां वयं शरणं गताः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: namaste sarvabhūtātman sarvaśaktidharāvyaya viśvakarman namaste
translation: We bow to you who are the Inner Controller of all creatures, O omnipotent and immutable Maker of all! We have sought asylum in you. We offer our salutations to you.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं प्रपन्नैः संविग्नैः वेपमानायनैर्बलः । प्रसादितः सुप्रसन्नो मा भैष्टेत्यभयं ददौ ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ prapannaiḥ saṃvignaiḥ vepamānāyanairbalaḥ prasāditaḥ suprasanno mā bhaiṣṭetyabhayaṃ dadau

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Balarāma was thus propitiated to be gracious (unto them) by Kauravas who sought his refuge, for they were terrified as their capital city was rocking. Being highly pleased with them, he assured them protection with the words, ‘Do not fear’.

devanagari: दुर्योधनः पारिबर्हं कुञ्जरान् षष्टिहायनान् । ददौ च द्वादशशतानि अयुतानि तुरङ्गमान् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: duryodhanaḥ pāribarhaṃ kuñjarān ṣaṣṭihāyanān dadau ca dvādaśaśatāni ayutāni turaṅgamān

translation: Duryodhana was very affectionate to his daughter. As presents, he (Duryodhana) gave twelve hundred elephants, each sixty years old (in its prime of youth) and ten thousand horses, six thousand gold-plated chariots shining like the sun and one thousand maid-servants each adorned with gold necklaces.

devanagari: रथानां षट्सहस्राणि रौक्माणां सूर्यवर्चसाम् । दासीनां निष्ककण्ठीनां सहस्रं दुहितृवत्सलः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: rathānāṃ ṣaṭsahasrāṇi raukmāṇāṃ sūryavarcasām dāsīnāṃ niṣkakaṇṭhīnāṃ sahasraṃ duhitṛvatsalaḥ
translation: Duryodhana was very affectionate to his daughter. As presents, he (Duryodhana) gave twelve hundred elephants, each sixty years old (in its prime of youth) and ten thousand horses, six thousand gold-plated chariots shining like the sun and one thousand maid-servants each adorned with gold necklaces.

devanagari: प्रतिगृह्य तु तत्सर्वं भगवान् सात्वतर्षभः । ससुतः सस्नुषः प्रायात् सुहृद्भिरभिनन्दितः ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: pratigṛhya tu tatsarvaṃ bhagavān sātvatarṣabhaḥ sasutaḥ sasnuṣaḥ prāyāt suhṛdbhirabhinanditaḥ
translation: Glorious Balarāma, the prominent Sātvata, accepted it all and complimented by his friends and well-wishers, he set out with his son (Sāmba) and the daughter-in-law.

devanagari: ततः प्रविष्टः स्वपुरं हलायुधः । समेत्य बन्धूननुरक्तचेतसः । शशंस सर्वं यदुपुङ्गवानां । मध्ये सभायां कुरुषु स्वचेष्टितम् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ praviṣṭaḥ svapuraṃ halāyudhaḥ sametya bandhūnanuraktacetasaḥ śaśaṃsa sarvaṃ yadupuṅgavānāṃ madhye sabhāyāṃ kuruṣu svaceṣṭitam

translation: Returning to his capital Dvārakā, Balarāma met all his affectionate relatives and in the assembly of prominent Yādavas narrated what he did in the Kaurava country.

devanagari: अद्यापि च पुरं ह्येतत् सूचयद् रामविक्रमम् । समुन्नतं दक्षिणतो गङ्गायां अनुदृश्यते ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: adyāpi ca puraṃ hyetat sūcayad rāmavikramam samunnataṃ dakṣiṇato gaṅgāyāṃ anudṛśyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sūc class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ sūc class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ sūc class 10 verb]

translation:
Even to this day the city shows the evidence of the exploit of Balarāma as it is elevated to the South and slopes down towards the Gaṅgā.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । हस्तिनपुरकर्षणरूपसंकर्षणविजयो नाम अष्टषष्टितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ६...
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe hastinapurakarṣaṇarūpasaṃkarṣaṇavijayo nāma aṣṭaṣaṣṭitamo
